title: Symphyotrichum ciliatum
intro: Symphyotrichum ciliatum, also known as the hairy white aster or silky aster, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and hairy stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Bioactive constituents of Symphyotrichum ciliatum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Symphyotrichum ciliatum is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it is also used in salves and creams for topical applications.
taxonomy: Symphyotrichum ciliatum is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, consisting of over 23,000 species. Within the family Asteraceae, Symphyotrichum ciliatum is further classified as belonging to the subfamily Asteroideae, and the tribe Astereae. The genus Symphyotrichum is a group of flowering plants native to North and South America, and is often referred to as the "ragged robes" or "downy asters." The species Symphyotrichum ciliatum is native to the western regions of North America.
uses: Symphyotrichum ciliatum has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly by the indigenous communities in North America. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are used to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Symphyotrichum ciliatum for medicinal purposes.

properties: Symphyotrichum ciliatum, also known as the hairy skeletonweed, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are used to create infusions, decoctions, and tinctures that are said to aid in reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
preparations: Symphyotrichum ciliatum, also known as the slender coneflower, is not commonly used in herbal preparations due to limited research and availability.
